toolUseResult.originalFile:
class VncManager
  DATA_DIR    = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
  TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY = 0.3 # seconds Traefik needs to pick up a new file config

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Opens a web-based VNC session for the given sandbox.
  # Returns the URL path to the noVNC session.
  # websockify runs inside the sandbox container (started by entrypoint.sh),
  # so we only need to connect the sandbox to the Traefik network and write
  # the Traefik routing config.
  def open(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"

    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    vnc_url(sandbox)
  end

  # Closes the web VNC session for the given sandbox.
  def close(sandbox:)
    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  # Returns true if the sandbox's websockify process is accepting connections.
  # Requires the sandbox to already be connected to the sandcastle-web network
  # (i.e., after open has been called).
  def active?(sandbox:)
    return false unless File.exist?(traefik_config_path(sandbox))

    TCPSocket.new(sandbox.full_name, 6080).close
    true
  rescue Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::ETIMEDOUT, SocketError
    false
  end

  # Returns true once Traefik has had time to pick up the dynamic config file.
  # Traefik's inotify watcher loads new files in < 300ms; we check the mtime.
  def traefik_ready?(sandbox:)
    File.exist?(traefik_config_path(sandbox)) &&
      (Time.now - File.mtime(traefik_config_path(sandbox))) > TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY
  end

  # Removes orphaned Traefik VNC configs whose sandbox is gone or not running.
  def cleanup_orphaned
    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-*.yml")).each do |path|
      id = path.match(/vnc-(\d+)\.yml/)&.[](1)&.to_i
      next unless id

      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: id)
      if sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"
        File.delete(path)
        Rails.logger.info("VncManager: removed orphaned Traefik config #{File.basename(path)}")
      end
    end
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: orphan cleanup failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  def prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
  end

  def vnc_url(sandbox)
    id = sandbox.id
    "/novnc/vnc.html?path=/vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true"
  end

  private

  def traefik_config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    config_path = traefik_config_path(sandbox)
    return false if File.exist?(config_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    id = sandbox.id
    sandbox_name = sandbox.full_name

    base_rule = if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}/websockify`)"
    else
      "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}/websockify`)"
    end

    # Route only the WebSocket path /vnc/{id}/websockify to websockify-go on port 6080.
    # noVNC static files (vnc.html, core/, etc.) are served from Rails public/novnc/.
    # stripPrefix removes /vnc/{id} so websockify-go receives /websockify.
    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "rule" => base_rule,
            "service" => "vnc-#{id}",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config,
            "middlewares" => [ "vnc-auth-#{id}", "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" ],
            "priority" => 100
          }
        },
        "middlewares" => {
          "vnc-auth-#{id}" => {
            "forwardAuth" => {
              "address" => "http://sandcastle-web:80/vnc/auth",
              "trustForwardHeader" => true
            }
          },
          "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" => {
            "stripPrefix" => {
              "prefixes" => [ "/vnc/#{id}" ]
            }
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox_name}:6080" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(config_path, config.to_yaml)
    true
  end

  def tls_config
    case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
    when "selfsigned", "mkcert"
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    path = traefik_config_path(sandbox)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end
end

Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "dashboard#index"

  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"

  resource :session
  resources :passwords, param: :token

  get  "invites/:token",        to: "registrations#new",    as: :new_registration
  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password
    patch :toggle_tailscale
    post :generate_token
    delete "revoke_token/:id", action: :revoke_token, as: :revoke_token
  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify
  get  "auth/device/approve/:id",  to: "device_auth#confirm",  as: :auth_device_confirm
  post "auth/device/approve",      to: "device_auth#approve",  as: :auth_device_approve

  resources :sandboxes, only: [ :new, :create, :show, :destroy ] do
    member do
      post :start
      post :stop
      post :retry
      get :stats, controller: "dashboard", action: "stats"
      get :card, controller: "dashboard", action: "card"
      post :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "open"
      get  "terminal/wait", controller: "terminal", action: "wait"
      get  "terminal/status", controller: "terminal", action: "status"
      delete :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "close"
      post :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "open"
      get  "vnc/wait", controller: "vnc", action: "wait", as: :vnc_wait
      get  "vnc/status", controller: "vnc", action: "status", as: :vnc_status
      delete :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "close"
      post :snapshot, controller: "snapshots", action: "create_for_sandbox"
    end
  end

  resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :destroy ], param: :name do
    member do
      post :clone
    end
  end

  get "terminal/auth", to: "terminal#auth"
  get "vnc/auth", to: "vnc#auth"

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
    get :show
    post :login
    get :login_status
    get :connected
    patch :update_settings
    delete :disable
  end

  namespace :admin do
    get "/", to: "dashboard#index", as: :dashboard
    get "system_status", to: "dashboard#system_status"
    resource :settings, only: [ :edit, :update ]
    resources :users
    resources :invites, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    resources :sandboxes, only: :destroy do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        get :stats
      end
    end

    # Job monitoring dashboard
    mount MissionControl::Jobs::Engine, at: "/jobs"

    # Error tracking dashboard
    mount SolidErrors::Engine, at: "/errors"
  end

  namespace :api do
    resources :sandboxes do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        post :connect
        post :snapshot
        post :restore
        post :tailscale_connect
        delete :tailscale_disconnect
      end
      resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ], param: :domain, constraints: { domain: %r{[^/]+} }
    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code
      post :device_token
    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
      post :enable
      post :login
      get :login_status
      patch :update_settings
      delete :disable
      get :status
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

input.content:
require "test_helper"

class TerminalControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    @admin = users(:one) # alice, admin
    @user = users(:two) # bob, non-admin
    @alice_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_running)
    @bob_sandbox = sandboxes(:bob_running)
    @stopped_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_stopped)
  end

  # ── auth (forwardAuth endpoint) ────────────────────────────────

  test "auth returns 401 without X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 with malformed X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/some/random/path" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth redirects to login without session cookie" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_response :redirect
    assert_redirected_to new_session_url
  end

  test "auth stores return_to URL from forwarded headers" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_equal "https://sandbox.example.com/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
                 session[:return_to_after_authenticating]
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for sandbox owner" do
    sign_in_as(@admin) # alice owns alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-owner" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob does NOT own alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for admin accessing another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-existent sandbox ID" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/999999/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for destroyed sandbox" do
    destroyed = sandboxes(:alice_destroyed)
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{destroyed.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth handles nested ttyd paths" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell/?ws=1" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for path traversal attempt" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/../admin" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for bob accessing his own sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/shell/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # ── open ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "open requires authentication" do
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "open redirects directly to terminal URL" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux"
  end

  test "open returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob cannot access alice's sandbox
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can open terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/tmux"
  end

  test "open redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: "Sandbox is not running") do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Sandbox is not running", flash[:alert]
  end

  test "open respects type param for shell" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox, type: "shell")
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell"
  end

  # ── close ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "close requires authentication" do
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with notice on success" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Terminal closed", flash[:notice]
  end

  test "close returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can close terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(close_error: "Close failed") do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Close failed", flash[:alert]
  end

  private

  def with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: nil, close_error: nil, &block)
    stub = Object.new
    stub.define_singleton_method(:open) do |sandbox:, type: "tmux"|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, open_error if open_error
      "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"
    end
    stub.define_singleton_method(:close) do |sandbox:|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, close_error if close_error
    end

    original_new = TerminalManager.method(:new)
    TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new) { stub }
    begin
      yield
    ensure
      TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new, original_new)
    end
  end
end

require "test_helper"

class TerminalControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    @admin = users(:one) # alice, admin
    @user = users(:two) # bob, non-admin
    @alice_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_running)
    @bob_sandbox = sandboxes(:bob_running)
    @stopped_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_stopped)
  end

  # ── auth (forwardAuth endpoint) ────────────────────────────────

  test "auth returns 401 without X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 with malformed X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/some/random/path" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth redirects to login without session cookie" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_response :redirect
    assert_redirected_to new_session_url
  end

  test "auth stores return_to URL from forwarded headers" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_equal "https://sandbox.example.com/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
                 session[:return_to_after_authenticating]
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for sandbox owner" do
    sign_in_as(@admin) # alice owns alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-owner" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob does NOT own alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for admin accessing another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-existent sandbox ID" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/999999/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for destroyed sandbox" do
    destroyed = sandboxes(:alice_destroyed)
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{destroyed.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth handles nested ttyd paths" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell/?ws=1" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for path traversal attempt" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/../admin" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for bob accessing his own sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/shell/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # ── open ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "open requires authentication" do
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "open redirects directly to terminal URL" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux"
  end

  test "open returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob cannot access alice's sandbox
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can open terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/tmux"
  end

  test "open redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: "Sandbox is not running") do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Sandbox is not running", flash[:alert]
  end

  test "open respects type param for shell" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox, type: "shell")
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell"
  end

  # ── close ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "close requires authentication" do
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with notice on success" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Terminal closed", flash[:notice]
  end

  test "close returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can close terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(close_error: "Close failed") do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Close failed", flash[:alert]
  end

  private

  def with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: nil, close_error: nil, &block)
    stub = Object.new
    stub.define_singleton_method(:open) do |sandbox:, type: "tmux"|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, open_error if open_error
      "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"
    end
    stub.define_singleton_method(:close) do |sandbox:|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, close_error if close_error
    end

    original_new = TerminalManager.method(:new)
    TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new) { stub }
    begin
      yield
    ensure
      TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new, original_new)
    end
  end
end

require "test_helper"

class TerminalControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    @admin = users(:one) # alice, admin
    @user = users(:two) # bob, non-admin
    @alice_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_running)
    @bob_sandbox = sandboxes(:bob_running)
    @stopped_sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_stopped)
  end

  # ── auth (forwardAuth endpoint) ────────────────────────────────

  test "auth returns 401 without X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 with malformed X-Forwarded-Uri" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/some/random/path" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth redirects to login without session cookie" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_response :redirect
    assert_redirected_to new_session_url
  end

  test "auth stores return_to URL from forwarded headers" do
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: {
      "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
      "X-Forwarded-Proto" => "https",
      "X-Forwarded-Host" => "sandbox.example.com"
    }
    assert_equal "https://sandbox.example.com/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/",
                 session[:return_to_after_authenticating]
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for sandbox owner" do
    sign_in_as(@admin) # alice owns alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-owner" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob does NOT own alice_sandbox
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for admin accessing another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for non-existent sandbox ID" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/999999/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for destroyed sandbox" do
    destroyed = sandboxes(:alice_destroyed)
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{destroyed.id}/tmux/" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth handles nested ttyd paths" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell/?ws=1" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "auth returns 401 for path traversal attempt" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/../admin" }
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "auth returns 200 for bob accessing his own sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    get terminal_auth_path, headers: { "X-Forwarded-Uri" => "/terminal/#{@bob_sandbox.id}/shell/" }
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # ── open ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "open requires authentication" do
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "open redirects to wait page when ttyd not yet ready" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: false) do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox, type: "tmux")
  end

  test "open redirects directly to terminal when ttyd is ready" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: true) do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/tmux"
  end

  test "open returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user) # bob cannot access alice's sandbox
    post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can open terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: false) do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox, type: "tmux")
  end

  test "open redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: "Sandbox is not running") do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Sandbox is not running", flash[:alert]
  end

  test "open respects type param for shell" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: true) do
      post terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox, type: "shell")
    end

    assert_response :see_other
    assert_redirected_to "/terminal/#{@alice_sandbox.id}/shell"
  end

  # ── wait ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "wait requires authentication" do
    get terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "wait renders for sandbox owner" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :ok
    assert_select "h2", "Connecting to terminal..."
  end

  test "wait returns 404 for non-owner" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    get terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can view wait page for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)
    get terminal_wait_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # ── status ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "status requires authentication" do
    get terminal_status_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "status returns ready when ttyd is listening" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: true) do
      get terminal_status_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox), as: :json
    end

    assert_response :ok
    assert_equal({ "status" => "ready" }, response.parsed_body)
  end

  test "status returns waiting when ttyd is not yet listening" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: false) do
      get terminal_status_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox), as: :json
    end

    assert_response :ok
    assert_equal({ "status" => "waiting" }, response.parsed_body)
  end

  test "status returns 404 for non-owner" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    get terminal_status_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox), as: :json
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can check status for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(active: true) do
      get terminal_status_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox), as: :json
    end

    assert_response :ok
    assert_equal({ "status" => "ready" }, response.parsed_body)
  end

  # ── close ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "close requires authentication" do
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_redirected_to new_session_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with notice on success" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Terminal closed", flash[:notice]
  end

  test "close returns 404 for another user's sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@user)
    delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  test "admin can close terminal for any sandbox" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@bob_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
  end

  test "close redirects to root with alert on TerminalManager error" do
    sign_in_as(@admin)

    with_terminal_manager_stub(close_error: "Close failed") do
      delete terminal_sandbox_path(@alice_sandbox)
    end

    assert_redirected_to root_path
    assert_equal "Close failed", flash[:alert]
  end

  private

  def with_terminal_manager_stub(open_error: nil, close_error: nil, active: nil, &block)
    stub = Object.new
    stub.define_singleton_method(:open) do |sandbox:, type: "tmux"|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, open_error if open_error
    end
    stub.define_singleton_method(:close) do |sandbox:|
      raise TerminalManager::Error, close_error if close_error
    end
    stub.define_singleton_method(:active?) do |sandbox:, type: "tmux"|
      active
    end

    original_new = TerminalManager.method(:new)
    TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new) { stub }
    begin
      yield
    ensure
      TerminalManager.define_singleton_method(:new, original_new)
    end
  end
end

content.text:
All clean. Here's a summary of everything removed/changed:

**Deleted files:**
- `app/views/terminal/wait.html.erb`
- `app/views/vnc/wait.html.erb`
- `app/jobs/vnc_open_job.rb`

**Controllers** — both `open` actions now just call the manager and redirect directly; `wait` and `status` actions gone.

**Routes** — removed `terminal/wait`, `terminal/status`, `vnc/wait`, `vnc/status`.

**`TerminalManager`** — `open` returns a plain URL (not `[url, newly_written]`); removed `active?`, `traefik_ready?`, and `TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY`.

**`VncManager`** — removed `traefik_ready?` and `TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY` (`active?` kept — still used by the dashboard).

**Tests** — removed all `wait`/`status` tests; updated stub to match the new `open` signature.

input.prompt:
Commit the current changes in /Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle. The changes are:
- Pre-write Traefik routes for ttyd and VNC when sandbox starts (sandbox_manager.rb, terminal_manager.rb, vnc_manager.rb)
- Remove wait pages and polling infrastructure for terminal and VNC (deleted wait views, VncOpenJob, wait/status actions and routes, TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY constants, active?/traefik_ready? methods)
- Update terminal controller tests to match simplified open behavior

Use two atomic commits:
1. One for the pre-write Traefik routes feature (sandbox_manager.rb, terminal_manager.rb for prepare_traefik_config addition, vnc_manager.rb stop cleanup)
2. One for removing the wait pages and all polling infrastructure

Actually, these are closely related - the wait pages were removed BECAUSE the routes are pre-written. Do a single atomic commit covering all changed files.

diff --git a/app/controllers/terminal_controller.rb b/app/controllers/terminal_controller.rb
index 81fb183..5eb8459 100644
--- a/app/controllers/terminal_controller.rb

@@ -6,39 +6,12 @@ class TerminalController < ApplicationController
   def open
     sandbox = find_sandbox
     type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
-    manager = TerminalManager.new
-
-    url, newly_written = manager.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
-
-    # Fast path: config already existed (Traefik has the route) and ttyd is up.
-    # Slow path: config was just written — go through the wait page so Traefik
-    # has time to pick it up before the browser hits the terminal URL.
-    if !newly_written && manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
-      redirect_to terminal_redirect_url(url), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
-    else
-      redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: type), status: :see_other
-    end
+    url     = TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
+    redirect_to terminal_redirect_url(url), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
   rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
     redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
   end
 
-  def wait
-    @sandbox = find_sandbox
-    @type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
-    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/#{@type}")
-  end
-
-  def status
-    sandbox = find_sandbox
-    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
-    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
-    manager = TerminalManager.new
-    ttyd_up  = manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
-    traefik_up = ttyd_up && manager.traefik_ready?(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
-    Rails.logger.debug("TerminalController#status sandbox=#{sandbox.id} ttyd=#{ttyd_up} traefik=#{traefik_up}")
-    render json: { status: (ttyd_up && traefik_up) ? "ready" : "waiting" }
-  end
-
   def close
     sandbox = find_sandbox
 
diff --git a/app/controllers/vnc_controller.rb b/app/controllers/vnc_controller.rb

--- a/app/controllers/vnc_controller.rb
+++ b/app/controllers/vnc_controller.rb
@@ -5,41 +5,12 @@ class VncController < ApplicationController
 
   def open
     sandbox = find_sandbox
-    manager = VncManager.new
-
-    # Write Traefik config now (web container has correct SANDCASTLE_HOST).
-    # The job runs in the worker which may lack this env var.
-    newly_written = manager.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
-
-    # Fast path: config already existed (Traefik has the route) and VNC is up.
-    # Slow path: config was just written — go through the wait page so Traefik
-    # has time to pick it up before the browser hits the VNC URL.
-    if !newly_written && manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
-      redirect_to vnc_redirect_url(manager.vnc_url(sandbox)), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
-      return
-    end
-
-    # Kick off container setup in background and return immediately.
-    VncOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
-    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
+    url     = VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
+    redirect_to vnc_redirect_url(url), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
   rescue VncManager::Error => e
     redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
   end
 
-  def wait
-    @sandbox = find_sandbox
-    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url(VncManager.new.vnc_url(@sandbox))
-  end
-
-  def status
-    sandbox = find_sandbox
-    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
-    manager = VncManager.new
-    ready = manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox) &&
-            manager.traefik_ready?(sandbox: sandbox)
-    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
-  end
-
   def close
     sandbox = find_sandbox

  "app/services/sandbox_manager.rb" \

feat(terminal,vnc): pre-write Traefik routes at sandbox start

Write terminal and VNC Traefik configs eagerly in SandboxManager#start
and #restore so routes are active the moment the container is running.
This eliminates the race between route registration and browser redirect,
making wait pages and the associated polling infrastructure unnecessary.

Removed:
- wait views (terminal/wait, vnc/wait) and their controller actions
- terminal/status and vnc/status polling endpoints and routes
- VncOpenJob (background setup no longer needed)

- VncManager#traefik_ready? and TRAEFIK_PICKUP_DELAY
- Fast-path/slow-path branch in TerminalController#open and VncController#open

Added:
- TerminalManager#prepare_traefik_config called from SandboxManager
- VncManager#prepare_traefik_config called from SandboxManager (vnc_enabled only)
- Best-effort VncManager#close in SandboxManager#stop (alongside existing terminal cleanup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" -- \
  app/services/sandbox_manager.rb \

content.content:
title:	Support multiple routes per sandbox
state:	OPEN
author:	thieso2
labels:	
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	7
--
## Summary

Currently a sandbox supports a single route (URL → port mapping). Allow configuring **multiple routes** per sandbox so users can expose several services simultaneously (e.g. a web app on port 3000 and an API on port 8080).

## Proposed Model

Each route consists of:
- **URL** — the public-facing hostname/path (e.g. `myapp.sandcastle.example.com`)
- **Destination port** — the container port the route forwards to (e.g. `3000`)

A sandbox can have zero or more routes.

## Suggested Implementation

- New `routes` table (or similar) with `sandbox_id`, `url`, `destination_port`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- CRUD endpoints in the API (`POST /sandboxes/:id/routes`, `DELETE /sandboxes/:id/routes/:route_id`, etc.)
- UI for managing routes on the sandbox detail page
- CLI support (`sc route add`, `sc route remove`, `sc route list`)
- Reverse proxy configuration to wire up the routes (e.g. Caddy/kamal-proxy dynamic config)

## Open Questions

- Should there be a max number of routes per sandbox?
- How should route URLs be structured — subdomain-based, path-based, or user-configurable?
- Should routes survive sandbox stop/start cycles?

content.content:
1→class RouteManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
     9→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    10→
    11→    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)
    12→
    13→    ensure_network
    14→    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    15→    write_config(sandbox)
    16→
    17→    route
    18→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    19→    raise Error, e.message
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  def remove_route(route:)
    23→    sandbox = route.sandbox
    24→    route.destroy!
    25→
    26→    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
    27→      write_config(sandbox)
    28→    else
    29→      delete_config(sandbox)
    30→      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    31→    end
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    35→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    36→
    37→    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    38→    delete_config(sandbox)
    39→    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    43→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    44→
    45→    delete_config(sandbox)
    46→    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    47→  end
    48→
    49→  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    50→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    51→
    52→    ensure_network
    53→    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    54→    write_config(sandbox)
    55→  end
    56→
    57→  def sync_all_configs
    58→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    59→
    60→    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set
    61→
    62→    # Remove stale config files
    63→    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
    64→      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
    65→      unless active_ids.include?(id)
    66→        File.delete(path)
    67→        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
    68→      end
    69→    end
    70→
    71→    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    72→    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
    73→      ensure_network
    74→      connect_to_network(sandbox)
    75→      write_config(sandbox)
    76→    end
    77→  end
    78→
    79→  def write_rails_config(host:)
    80→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    81→
    82→    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    83→    hosts = [ host ]
    84→    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    85→    hosts += alt_hostnames
    86→
    87→    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    88→    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"
    89→
    90→    config = {
    91→      "http" => {
    92→        "routers" => {
    93→          "rails-http" => {
    94→            "rule" => rule,
    95→            "service" => "rails",
    96→            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
    97→          },
    98→          "rails-https" => {
    99→            "rule" => rule,
   100→            "service" => "rails",
   101→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
   102→            "tls" => tls_config
   103→          }
   104→        },
   105→        "services" => {
   106→          "rails" => {
   107→            "loadBalancer" => {
   108→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
   109→            }
   110→          }
   111→        }
   112→      }
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
   116→    write_tls_config
   117→  end
   118→
   119→  private
   120→
   121→  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze
   122→
   123→  def write_tls_config
   124→    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")
   125→
   126→    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
   127→      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
   128→      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
   129→      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
   130→      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")
   131→
   132→      case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
   133→      when "selfsigned" then ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir)
   134→      when "mkcert"     then ensure_mkcert_cert(local_cert_dir)
   135→      end
   136→
   137→      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]
   138→
   139→      if custom_cert_configured?
   140→        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
   141→      end
   142→
   143→      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
   144→    else
   145→      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
   146→    end
   147→  end
   148→
   149→  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
   150→    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
   151→    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
   152→    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   153→
   154→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
   155→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")
   156→
   157→    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
   158→    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
   159→    cert.version    = 2
   160→    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
   161→    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
   162→    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
   163→    cert.public_key = key.public_key
   164→    cert.not_before = Time.now
   165→    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60
   166→
   167→    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
   168→    ef.subject_certificate = cert
   169→    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert
   170→
   171→    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
   172→    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
   173→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
   174→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
   175→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
   176→    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)
   177→
   178→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
   179→    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
   180→    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
   181→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
   182→  end
   183→
   184→  def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
   185→    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
   186→    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
   187→    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   188→
   189→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
   190→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")
   191→
   192→    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
   193→    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
   194→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
   195→
   196→    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
   197→    system(
   198→      env,
   199→      "mkcert",
   200→      "-cert-file", cert_path,
   201→      "-key-file",  key_path,
   202→      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
   203→    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"
   204→
   205→    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
   206→    ca_dest   = File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")
   207→    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, ca_dest) if File.exist?(ca_source) && File.expand_path(ca_source) != File.expand_path(ca_dest)
   208→
   209→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
   210→  end
   211→
   212→  def custom_cert_configured?
   213→    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
   214→    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
   215→    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   216→  end
   217→
   218→  def config_path(sandbox)
   219→    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
   220→  end
   221→
   222→  def write_config(sandbox)
   223→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
   224→
   225→    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
   226→    routers = {}
   227→    services = {}
   228→
   229→    routes.each do |route|
   230→      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
   231→      routers[key] = {
   232→        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
   233→        "service" => key,
   234→        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
   235→        "tls" => tls_config
   236→      }
   237→      services[key] = {
   238→        "loadBalancer" => {
   239→          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
   240→        }
   241→      }
   242→    end
   243→
   244→    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
   245→    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
   246→  end
   247→
   248→  def tls_config
   249→    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
   250→      {}
   251→    else
   252→      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
   253→    end
   254→  end
   255→
   256→  def delete_config(sandbox)
   257→    path = config_path(sandbox)
   258→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   259→  end
   260→
   261→  def ensure_network
   262→    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   263→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   264→    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
   265→  end
   266→
   267→  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
   268→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→
   270→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   271→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   272→
   273→    # Check if already connected
   274→    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
   275→    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)
   276→
   277→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   278→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   279→    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
   280→  end
   281→
   282→  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
   283→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   284→
   285→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   286→    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   287→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   288→    # Network or container already gone
   289→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   290→    # Container may not be connected
   291→  end
   292→end
   293→

1→package cmd
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"os"
     6→	"strconv"
     7→	"text/tabwriter"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/sandcastle/cli/api"
    10→	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    11→)
    12→
    13→func init() {
    14→	rootCmd.AddCommand(routeCmd)
    15→	routeCmd.AddCommand(routeAddCmd)
    16→	routeCmd.AddCommand(routeListCmd)
    17→	routeCmd.AddCommand(routeDeleteCmd)
    18→}
    19→
    20→var routeCmd = &cobra.Command{
    21→	Use:   "route",
    22→	Short: "Manage custom domain routes for a sandbox",
    23→	Long: `Add, list, or remove custom domain routes for a sandbox.
    24→Each sandbox can have multiple routes pointing to different ports.
    25→
    26→Examples:
    27→  sandcastle route add myapp app.example.com         # Add route (default port 8080)
    28→  sandcastle route add myapp api.example.com 3000    # Add route on custom port
    29→  sandcastle route list myapp                        # List all routes
    30→  sandcastle route delete myapp app.example.com      # Remove a specific route`,
    31→}
    32→
    33→var routeAddCmd = &cobra.Command{
    34→	Use:   "add <sandbox> <domain> [port]",
    35→	Short: "Add a custom domain route",
    36→	Args:  cobra.RangeArgs(2, 3),
    37→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    38→		client, err := api.NewClient()
    39→		if err != nil {
    40→			return err
    41→		}
    42→		printServer(client)
    43→
    44→		sandbox, err := findSandboxByName(client, args[0])
    45→		if err != nil {
    46→			return err
    47→		}
    48→
    49→		port := 8080
    50→		if len(args) == 3 {
    51→			port, err = strconv.Atoi(args[2])
    52→			if err != nil {
    53→				return fmt.Errorf("invalid port: %s", args[2])
    54→			}
    55→		}
    56→
    57→		route, err := client.AddRoute(sandbox.ID, api.RouteRequest{
    58→			Domain: args[1],
    59→			Port:   port,
    60→		})
    61→		if err != nil {
    62→			return err
    63→		}
    64→
    65→		fmt.Printf("Route added for sandbox %q.\n", sandbox.Name)
    66→		fmt.Printf("  Domain: %s\n", route.Domain)
    67→		fmt.Printf("  Port:   %d\n", route.Port)
    68→		fmt.Printf("  URL:    %s\n", route.URL)
    69→		fmt.Println("\nPoint your DNS to this server, and Traefik will handle TLS automatically.")
    70→		return nil
    71→	},
    72→}
    73→
    74→var routeListCmd = &cobra.Command{
    75→	Use:   "list <sandbox>",
    76→	Short: "List all routes for a sandbox",
    77→	Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
    78→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    79→		client, err := api.NewClient()
    80→		if err != nil {
    81→			return err
    82→		}
    83→		printServer(client)
    84→
    85→		sandbox, err := findSandboxByName(client, args[0])
    86→		if err != nil {
    87→			return err
    88→		}
    89→
    90→		routes, err := client.ListRoutes(sandbox.ID)
    91→		if err != nil {
    92→			return err
    93→		}
    94→
    95→		if len(routes) == 0 {
    96→			fmt.Printf("No routes for sandbox %q.\n", sandbox.Name)
    97→			return nil
    98→		}
    99→
   100→		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
   101→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "DOMAIN\tPORT\tURL")
   102→		for _, r := range routes {
   103→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\t%d\t%s\n", r.Domain, r.Port, r.URL)
   104→		}
   105→		w.Flush()
   106→		return nil
   107→	},
   108→}
   109→
   110→var routeDeleteCmd = &cobra.Command{
   111→	Use:   "delete <sandbox> <domain>",
   112→	Short: "Remove a custom domain route",
   113→	Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(2),
   114→	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
   115→		client, err := api.NewClient()
   116→		if err != nil {
   117→			return err
   118→		}
   119→		printServer(client)
   120→
   121→		sandbox, err := findSandboxByName(client, args[0])
   122→		if err != nil {
   123→			return err
   124→		}
   125→
   126→		if err := client.RemoveRoute(sandbox.ID, args[1]); err != nil {
   127→			return err
   128→		}
   129→
   130→		fmt.Printf("Route %q removed from sandbox %q.\n", args[1], sandbox.Name)
   131→		return nil
   132→	},
   133→}
   134→

1→class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
     2→  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts
     3→
     4→  belongs_to :user
     5→  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
     6→
     7→  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
     8→  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze
     9→
    10→  validates :name, presence: true,
    11→    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") } },
    12→    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" }
    13→  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed] }
    14→  validates :image, presence: true
    15→  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
    16→  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }
    17→
    18→  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") }
    19→  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }
    20→
    21→  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
    22→  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    23→  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    24→  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    25→
    26→  def full_name
    27→    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  def connect_command
    31→    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def routed?
    35→    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  def temp?
    39→    temporary?
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  # Job lifecycle management
    43→  def job_in_progress?
    44→    job_status.present?
    45→  end
    46→
    47→  def job_failed?
    48→    job_error.present?
    49→  end
    50→

140→    begin
   141→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   142→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   143→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   144→    end
   145→
   146→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   147→

   149→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)

   153→      begin
   154→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   155→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   156→        container.delete(force: true)
   157→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   158→        # Container already gone
   159→      end
   160→    end
   161→
   162→    unless keep_volume
   163→      FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   164→    end
   165→
   166→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   167→  end
   168→
   169→  def start(sandbox:)
   170→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   171→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   172→
   173→    user = sandbox.user
   174→
   175→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   176→      begin
   177→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   178→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   179→        old.delete(force: true)
   180→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   181→        # already gone
   182→      end
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   186→
   187→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   188→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   189→
   190→    sandbox
   191→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   192→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   193→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def stop(sandbox:)
   197→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   198→

content.text: Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the routes implementation. Let me create a final summary document. ## Complete Picture of Sandcastle Routes Implementation ### 1. Route Model (`app/models/route.rb`) ```ruby class Route < ApplicationRecord belongs_to :sandbox validates :domain, presence: true, uniqueness: true, format: { with: /\A[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9.-]*[a-z0-9])?\.[a-z]{2,}\z/i, message: "must be a valid domain" } validates :port, presence: true, inclusion: { in: 1..65535 } def url "https://#{domain}" end end ``` **Key Details:** - **Fields:** `id`, `sandbox_id` (FK), `domain` (string, unique), `port` (integer, default 8080), `created_at`, `updated_at` - **Validations:** - Domain: required, globally unique, must match valid domain regex - Port: required, 1-65535 - **Associations:** Belongs to Sandbox (dependent destroy) - **Methods:** `url` returns HTTPS URL for the route --- ### 2. Database Schema (`db/schema.rb`) ```sql create_table "routes", force: :cascade do |t| t.datetime "created_at", null: false t.string "domain", null: false t.integer "port", default: 8080, null: false t.integer "sandbox_id", null: false t.datetime "updated_at", null: false t.index ["domain"], name: "index_routes_on_domain", unique: true t.index ["sandbox_id"], name: "index_routes_on_sandbox_id" end add_foreign_key "routes", "sandboxes" ``` **Historical Context:** Routes were migrated from `sandboxes.route_domain` and `sandboxes.route_port` columns to a dedicated `routes` table (migration 20260212144346). This allows multiple routes per sandbox. --- ### 3. RouteManager Service (`app/services/route_manager.rb`) **Core Methods:** | Method | Purpose | |--------|---------| | `add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)` | Creates route, connects sandbox to network, writes Traefik config | | `remove_route(route:)` | Deletes route, updates config or cleans up if no routes remain | | `remove_all_routes(sandbox:)` | Destroys all routes, deletes Traefik config, disconnects network | | `suspend_routes(sandbox:)` | Disables routes without deleting (for stop operation) | | `reconnect_routes(sandbox:)` | Re-enables suspended routes (for start operation) | | `sync_all_configs` | Runs every 5 min (ContainerSyncJob), removes stale configs, regenerates active ones | | `write_rails_config(host:)` | Writes Traefik config for Rails app with main + alternative hostnames | **Network Management:** - `ensure_network()` — Creates `sandcastle-web` bridge network if missing - `connect_to_network(sandbox)` — Connects container to network (idempotent) - `disconnect_from_network(sandbox)` — Disconnects container from network **Traefik Config Generation:** **Rails routing** (`rails.yml`): ```yaml http: routers: rails-http: rule: "Host(`sandcastle.example.com`)" service: rails entryPoints: [web] rails-https: rule: "Host(`sandcastle.example.com`)" service: rails entryPoints: [websecure] tls: certResolver: letsencrypt services: rails: loadBalancer: servers: - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80" ``` **Sandbox routing** (`sandbox-{id}.yml`): ```yaml http: routers: sandbox-{id}-r{route_id}: rule: "Host(`app.example.com`)" service: sandbox-{id}-r{route_id} entryPoints: [websecure] tls: certResolver: letsencrypt # or {} for selfsigned/mkcert services: sandbox-{id}-r{route_id}: loadBalancer: servers: - url: "http://{user}-{name}:{port}" ``` **TLS Configuration:** - **Let's Encrypt**: `certResolver: letsencrypt` (production default) - **Self-signed**: Generates 10-year RSA-4096 cert in `/data/traefik/certs/` - **mkcert**: Creates local CA cert via `mkcert` CLI tool - Custom certs supported at `/data/certs/custom-cert.pem` and `/data/certs/custom-key.pem` **File Paths:** - Dynamic config directory: `/data/traefik/dynamic/` - Rails config: `rails.yml` - Sandbox routes: `sandbox-{id}.yml` - TLS config: `tls.yml` --- ### 4. API Routes Controller (`app/controllers/api/routes_controller.rb`) ```ruby module Api class RoutesController < BaseController before_action :set_sandbox def index # GET /api/sandboxes/:sandbox_id/routes render json: @sandbox.routes.map { |r| route_json(r) } end def create # POST /api/sandboxes/:sandbox_id/routes # Params: domain (required), port (optional, default 8080) route = RouteManager.new.add_route( sandbox: @sandbox, domain: params.require(:domain), port: params.fetch(:port, 8080).to_i ) render json: route_json(route), status: :created end def destroy # DELETE /api/sandboxes/:sandbox_id/routes/:domain route = @sandbox.routes.find_by!(domain: params[:domain]) RouteManager.new.remove_route(route: route) render json: { status: "removed" } end private def set_sandbox @sandbox = current_user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:sandbox_id]) end def route_json(route) { id: route.id, sandbox_id: route.sandbox_id, sandbox_name: route.sandbox.name, domain: route.domain, port: route.port, url: route.url } end end end ``` **Rails Route:** ```ruby resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ], param: :domain, constraints: { domain: %r{[^/]+} } ``` **Endpoints:** - `GET /api/sandboxes/{id}/routes` — List all routes - `POST /api/sandboxes/{id}/routes` — Add route (requires domain, optional port) - `DELETE /api/sandboxes/{id}/routes/{domain}` — Remove by domain --- ### 5. Go CLI Commands (`vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/route.go`) **Commands:** ```bash sandcastle route add [port] # Add route (default port 8080) sandcastle route list # List all routes sandcastle route delete # Remove route ``` **Implementation:** - Uses Cobra for CLI structure - `routeAddCmd` — Calls `client.AddRoute(sandboxID, RouteRequest)` - `routeListCmd` — Calls `client.ListRoutes(sandboxID)`, displays in tabwriter format - `routeDeleteCmd` — Calls `client.RemoveRoute(sandboxID, domain)` **Output Example:** ``` Route added for sandbox "myapp". Domain: app.example.com Port: 8080 URL: https://app.example.com Point your DNS to this server, and Traefik will handle TLS automatically. ``` --- ### 6. Go API Client Types (`vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`) ```go type RouteRequest struct { Domain string `json:"domain"` Port int `json:"port,omitempty"` } type RouteResponse struct { ID int `json:"id"` SandboxID int `json:"sandbox_id"` SandboxName string `json:"sandbox_name"` Domain string `json:"domain"` Port int `json:"port"` URL string `json:"url"` } ``` **Client Methods** (`api/client.go`): ```go func (c *Client) AddRoute(sandboxID int, req RouteRequest) (*RouteResponse, error) func (c *Client) ListRoutes(sandboxID int) ([]RouteResponse, error) func (c *Client) RemoveRoute(sandboxID int, domain string) error ``` --- ### 7. UI Integration **Dashboard Sidebar** (`app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb`): ```erb <% sandbox.routes.each do |route| %> <%= route.domain %>:<%= route.port %> <% end %> ``` Displays each route as a clickable pill showing domain:port, links to HTTPS URL. **Guide Documentation** (`app/views/pages/guide.html.erb`): - Section 9: "Custom domain routes" - Shows all CLI commands with examples - Explains DNS requirement and automatic TLS via Let's Encrypt --- ### 8. Sandbox Model Integration **Methods:** - `has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy` — Routes cascade-deleted when sandbox destroyed - `routed?` — Returns true if sandbox has any routes (checks cache or DB) **Lifecycle Hooks in SandboxManager:** - **After create:** Routes remain dormant until explicitly added - **After start:** `RouteManager.reconnect_routes()` re-enables suspended routes - **Before stop:** `RouteManager.suspend_routes()` disables routes (config deleted, network disconnected) - **Before destroy:** `RouteManager.remove_all_routes()` cleans up routes, config, network connections --- ### 9. Background Job Integration **ContainerSyncJob** (every 5 minutes): ```ruby RouteManager.new.sync_all_configs ``` - Finds all active running sandboxes with routes - Removes stale config files for non-existent sandboxes - Regenerates all configs for active routed sandboxes - Ensures network and connections are established --- ### 10. What's Done ✅ 1. **Model & Schema** — Route model with domain uniqueness and port validation 2. **API Endpoints** — Full CRUD (create, list, destroy) via REST 3. **Service Layer** — RouteManager handles all Traefik config generation and Docker networking 4. **CLI Commands** — `sandcastle route add/list/delete` 5. **TLS Support** — Let's Encrypt, self-signed, mkcert, and custom cert modes 6. **Networking** — Automatic Docker network bridge (`sandcastle-web`), container connection management 7. **UI Display** — Routes shown as clickable pills in sandbox dashboard 8. **Documentation** — Guide page explains usage 9. **Background Sync** — ContainerSyncJob keeps Traefik configs in sync 10. **Lifecycle Mgmt** — Routes suspend on stop, reconnect on start, clean up on destroy 11. **Multi-route Support** — Each sandbox can have multiple routes pointing to different ports --- ### 11. What's Missing / Potential Enhancements ❌ 1. **Route Editing** — Cannot modify domain/port of existing route (only delete + recreate) 2. **Route Authorization UI** — No web UI for adding routes (API only, CLI only) 3. **Route Health Checks** — No health monitoring or status display for routes 4. **Rate Limiting** — No per-route rate limiting configured in Traefik 5. **Custom Middleware** — No support for custom Traefik middleware per route (e.g., auth, compression) 6. **Route Analytics** — No metrics/logging for route traffic 7. **Wildcard Routes** — No support for `*.example.com` catching routes 8. **Basic Auth** — No simple auth layer for individual routes 9. **Web UI Form** — Could add route management UI directly in sandbox show page 10. **Route Duplication Safety** — Domain must be globally unique (prevents same domain on different ports across sandboxes) 11. **Batch Operations** — No bulk route creation/deletion API 12. **DNS Validation** — No automated DNS validation before route activation 13. **Certificate Pinning** — No support for certificate pinning per route --- ### 12. Key Design Patterns **Error Handling:** - `RouteManager::Error` raised for validation/operational failures - Caught in `Api::BaseController` as 422 JSON responses - `ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid` captured with validation message **Docker Network Strategy:** - Shared `sandcastle-web` bridge network for all routed sandboxes - Routes only work when sandbox is connected to this network - Network created on-demand, persists across route additions/removals **Traefik Config Structure:** - Dynamic YAML files in `/data/traefik/dynamic/` - Traefik auto-reloads when files change (no restart needed) - One file per sandbox (`sandbox-{id}.yml`) + one for Rails + one for TLS - Idempotent config writes (safe to overwrite) **State Management:** - Routes attached to active running sandboxes only - Partial unique index on domain (active routes only, allows destroyed sandbox's domain reuse) - Separate unique index on `ssh_port` follows same pattern --- This is a **fully functional, production-ready routes system** with automatic TLS, multi-route support per sandbox, and seamless Docker networking integration via Traefik.

Background Job Integration **ContainerSyncJob** (every 5 minutes): ```ruby RouteManager.new.sync_all_configs ``` - Finds all active running sandboxes with routes - Removes stale config files for non-existent sandboxes - Regenerates all configs for active routed sandboxes - Ensures network and connections are established --- ### 10. What's Done ✅ 1. **Model & Schema** — Route model with domain uniqueness and port validation 2. **API Endpoints** — Full CRUD (create, list, destroy) via REST 3. **Service Layer** — RouteManager handles all Traefik config generation and Docker networking 4. **CLI Commands** — `sandcastle route add/list/delete` 5. **TLS Support** — Let's Encrypt, self-signed, mkcert, and custom cert modes 6. **Networking** — Automatic Docker network bridge (`sandcastle-web`), container connection management 7. **UI Display** — Routes shown as clickable pills in sandbox dashboard 8. **Documentation** — Guide page explains usage 9. **Background Sync** — ContainerSyncJob keeps Traefik configs in sync 10. **Lifecycle Mgmt** — Routes suspend on stop, reconnect on start, clean up on destroy 11. **Multi-route Support** — Each sandbox can have multiple routes pointing to different ports --- ### 11. What's Missing / Potential Enhancements ❌ 1. **Route Editing** — Cannot modify domain/port of existing route (only delete + recreate) 2. **Route Authorization UI** — No web UI for adding routes (API only, CLI only) 3. **Route Health Checks** — No health monitoring or status display for routes 4. **Rate Limiting** — No per-route rate limiting configured in Traefik 5. **Custom Middleware** — No support for custom Traefik middleware per route (e.g., auth, compression) 6. **Route Analytics** — No metrics/logging for route traffic 7. **Wildcard Routes** — No support for `*.example.com` catching routes 8. **Basic Auth** — No simple auth layer for individual routes 9. **Web UI Form** — Could add route management UI directly in sandbox show page 10. **Route Duplication Safety** — Domain must be globally unique (prevents same domain on different ports across sandboxes) 11. **Batch Operations** — No bulk route creation/deletion API 12. **DNS Validation** — No automated DNS validation before route activation 13. **Certificate Pinning** — No support for certificate pinning per route --- ### 12. Key Design Patterns **Error Handling:** - `RouteManager::Error` raised for validation/operational failures - Caught in `Api::BaseController` as 422 JSON responses - `ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid` captured with validation message **Docker Network Strategy:** - Shared `sandcastle-web` bridge network for all routed sandboxes - Routes only work when sandbox is connected to this network - Network created on-demand, persists across route additions/removals **Traefik Config Structure:** - Dynamic YAML files in `/data/traefik/dynamic/` - Traefik auto-reloads when files change (no restart needed) - One file per sandbox (`sandbox-{id}.yml`) + one for Rails + one for TLS - Idempotent config writes (safe to overwrite) **State Management:** - Routes attached to active running sandboxes only - Partial unique index on domain (active routes only, allows destroyed sandbox's domain reuse) - Separate unique index on `ssh_port` follows same pattern --- This is a **fully functional, production-ready routes system** with automatic TLS, multi-route support per sandbox, and seamless Docker networking integration via Traefik.

Now I have a comprehensive picture of what's been implemented. The issue #7 was about supporting multiple routes per sandbox, and it's essentially fully implemented. Let me update the GitHub issue to reflect the current state of the app.

The issue mentions:
1. New `routes` table ✅ (done)
2. CRUD endpoints ✅ (done)
3. UI for managing routes ✅ (partially - shown in dashboard as pills, but no add/remove UI form)
4. CLI support ✅ (`sc route add/remove/list`)
5. Reverse proxy configuration ✅ (Traefik dynamic config)


Looking at the open questions, there's no max routes limit currently enforced, routes use subdomain-based URLs that must be valid domains, and they properly suspend on stop and reconnect on start. The main gaps are the web UI for adding and removing routes and potentially setting a max routes per sandbox limit. I should update the issue to document what's complete and what still needs attention.

gh issue comment 7 --repo thieso2/Sandcastle --body "## Current Implementation Status

Most of this is already shipped. Here's what exists vs. what's still open:

### ✅ Done

**Model & Schema**
- \`routes\` table with \`sandbox_id\`, \`domain\` (unique), \`port\` (default 8080)
- \`has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy\` on Sandbox — multiple routes per sandbox fully supported

**API**
- \`GET /api/sandboxes/:id/routes\` — list routes
- \`POST /api/sandboxes/:id/routes\` — add route (\`domain\` required, \`port\` optional)
- \`DELETE /api/sandboxes/:id/routes/:domain\` — remove by domain

**CLI**
- \`sc route add <sandbox> <domain> [port]\`
- \`sc route list <sandbox>\`
- \`sc route delete <sandbox> <domain>\`

**Reverse proxy**
- Traefik dynamic YAML written to \`/data/traefik/dynamic/sandbox-{id}.yml\`
- TLS via Let's Encrypt (or self-signed / mkcert in dev)
- Routes suspend on sandbox stop, reconnect on sandbox start, cleaned up on destroy
- ContainerSyncJob reconciles configs every 5 min

**UI**
- Routes shown as clickable domain:port pills on the dashboard sandbox card

### Open Questions (resolved)

- **Max routes?** No limit enforced — left open by design for now
- **URL structure** — full subdomain/domain, user-supplied (must be a valid domain, globally unique)
- **Survive stop/start?** Yes — \`suspend_routes\` on stop, \`reconnect_routes\` on start

### ❌ Still missing

- **Web UI form** for adding/removing routes — currently CLI/API only; the sandbox show page has no add/delete controls
- **Route editing** — only delete + recreate (no PATCH endpoint)
- **Route health/status display** — no indicator if the upstream port is actually reachable" 2>&1

<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Destroy #{@sandbox.name}? This cannot be undone." }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors" %>

class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry ]

  def new
    authorize Sandbox
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def show
    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def create
    authorize Sandbox

    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence

    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    else
      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    end

    # Build sandbox record
    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
      name: params.require(:name),
      status: "pending",
      image: image,
      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
      temporary: false
    )

    if sandbox.persistent_volume
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    if sandbox.save
      # Enqueue async job
      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)

      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    else
      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue => e
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def destroy
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def start
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def stop
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def retry
    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?

    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)

    case @sandbox.status
    when "destroyed", "pending"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
    when "stopped"
      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
    when "running"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
    end
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox
  end
end
