class RouteManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"

    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)

    route
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  def remove_route(route:)
    sandbox = route.sandbox
    route.destroy!

    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
      write_config(sandbox)
    else
      delete_config(sandbox)
      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    delete_config(sandbox)
    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
  end

  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    return unless sandbox.routed?

    ensure_network
    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    write_config(sandbox)
  end

  def sync_all_configs
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set

    # Remove stale config files
    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
      unless active_ids.include?(id)
        File.delete(path)
        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
      end
    end

    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
      ensure_network
      connect_to_network(sandbox)
      write_config(sandbox)
    end
  end

  def write_rails_config(host:)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    hosts = [ host ]
    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    hosts += alt_hostnames

    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "rails-http" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
          },
          "rails-https" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "rails",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "rails" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
    write_tls_config
  end

  private

  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze

  def write_tls_config
    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")

    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")

      case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
      when "selfsigned" then ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir)
      when "mkcert"     then ensure_mkcert_cert(local_cert_dir)
      end

      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]

      if custom_cert_configured?
        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
      end

      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
    else
      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
    end
  end

  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")

    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
    cert.version    = 2
    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
    cert.public_key = key.public_key
    cert.not_before = Time.now
    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60

    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
    ef.subject_certificate = cert
    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert

    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)

    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")

    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)

    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
    system(
      env,
      "mkcert",
      "-cert-file", cert_path,
      "-key-file",  key_path,
      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"

    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
    ca_dest   = File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")
    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, ca_dest) if File.exist?(ca_source) && File.expand_path(ca_source) != File.expand_path(ca_dest)

    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
  end

  def custom_cert_configured?
    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
  end

  def config_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
  end

  def write_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
    routers = {}
    services = {}

    routes.each do |route|
      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
      routers[key] = {
        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
        "service" => key,
        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
        "tls" => tls_config
      }
      services[key] = {
        "loadBalancer" => {
          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
        }
      }
    end

    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_config(sandbox)
    path = config_path(sandbox)
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Check if already connected
    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
  end

  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Network or container already gone
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    # Container may not be connected
  end
end

file.content:
class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy

  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") } },
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command
    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

class SandboxPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
  def index?                 = true
  def show?                  = owner_or_admin?
  def create?                = true
  def update?                = true
  def destroy?               = owner_or_admin?
  def start?                 = owner_or_admin?
  def stop?                  = owner_or_admin?
  def retry?                 = owner_or_admin?
  def stats?                 = owner_or_admin?
  def card?                  = owner_or_admin?
  def connect?               = owner_or_admin?
  def snapshot?              = owner_or_admin?
  def restore?               = owner_or_admin?
  def tailscale_connect?     = owner_or_admin?
  def tailscale_disconnect?  = owner_or_admin?

  private

  def owner_or_admin?
    admin? || record.user_id == user.id
  end

  class Scope < ApplicationPolicy::Scope
    def resolve
      if admin?
        scope.active
      else
        scope.where(user:).active
      end
    end
  end
end

input.content:
class RoutesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox

  def create
    RouteManager.new.add_route(
      sandbox: @sandbox,
      domain: params.require(:route).require(:domain).strip,
      port: params.require(:route).fetch(:port, 8080).to_i
    )
    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), notice: "Route added."
  rescue RouteManager::Error, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), alert: e.message
  end

  def destroy
    route = @sandbox.routes.find(params[:id])
    RouteManager.new.remove_route(route: route)
    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), notice: "Route removed."
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), alert: "Route not found."
  rescue RouteManager::Error => e
    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:sandbox_id])
    authorize @sandbox, :show?
  end
end

  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify
  get  "auth/device/approve/:id",  to: "device_auth#confirm",  as: :auth_device_confirm
  post "auth/device/approve",      to: "device_auth#approve",  as: :auth_device_approve

  resources :sandboxes, only: [ :new, :create, :show, :destroy ] do
    member do
      post :start
      post :stop
      post :retry
      get :stats, controller: "dashboard", action: "stats"
      get :card, controller: "dashboard", action: "card"
      post :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "open"
      delete :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "close"
      post :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "open"
      delete :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "close"
      post :snapshot, controller: "snapshots", action: "create_for_sandbox"
    end
  end

  resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :destroy ], param: :name do
    member do
      post :clone
    end
  end

  get "terminal/auth", to: "terminal#auth"
  get "vnc/auth", to: "vnc#auth"

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
    get :show
    post :login
    get :login_status
    get :connected
    patch :update_settings
    delete :disable
  end

  namespace :admin do
    get "/", to: "dashboard#index", as: :dashboard
    get "system_status", to: "dashboard#system_status"
    resource :settings, only: [ :edit, :update ]
    resources :users
    resources :invites, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    resources :sandboxes, only: :destroy do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        get :stats
      end
    end

    # Job monitoring dashboard
    mount MissionControl::Jobs::Engine, at: "/jobs"

    # Error tracking dashboard
    mount SolidErrors::Engine, at: "/errors"
  end

  namespace :api do
    resources :sandboxes do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        post :connect
        post :snapshot
        post :restore
        post :tailscale_connect
        delete :tailscale_disconnect
      end
      resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ], param: :domain, constraints: { domain: %r{[^/]+} }
    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code
      post :device_token
    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
      post :enable
      post :login
      get :login_status
      patch :update_settings
      delete :disable
      get :status
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry ]

  def new
    authorize Sandbox
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def show
    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def create
    authorize Sandbox

    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence

    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    else
      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    end

    # Build sandbox record
    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
      name: params.require(:name),
      status: "pending",
      image: image,
      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
      temporary: false
    )

    if sandbox.persistent_volume
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    if sandbox.save
      # Enqueue async job
      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)

      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    else
      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue => e
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def destroy
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def start
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def stop
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def retry
    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?

    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)

    case @sandbox.status
    when "destroyed", "pending"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
    when "stopped"
      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
    when "running"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
    end
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox
  end
end

<% content_for(:title) { "Getting Started — Sandcastle" } %>

Getting Started

Everything you need to go from zero to a running sandbox.

<% host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "sandcastle.example.com") %> <%# ── Install ── %>

1. Install the CLI

Download the latest release from <%= link_to "GitHub Releases", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %>, then extract it: 

tar xzf sandcastle_*.tar.gz
sudo mv sandcastle /usr/local/bin/
<%# ── Login ── %>

2. Log in

Point the CLI at this server and authenticate via your browser:

sandcastle login https://<%= host %>
<%# ── Tailscale ── %>

3. Enable Tailscale (recommended)

Each sandbox gets its own Tailscale IP so you can reach services directly — no port forwarding needed. 

# Interactive login — opens a browser URL to authenticate
sandcastle tailscale enable

# Or use an auth key for headless setups
sandcastle tailscale enable --auth-key tskey-auth-...

After authenticating, approve the advertised subnet routes in the Tailscale admin console.

<%# ── Create ── %>

4. Create a sandbox

Web UI — click the "Create Sandcastle" button on your dashboard to create a sandbox with all available options:

  • Name — unique identifier for your sandbox
  • Container Image — custom image or default
  • Snapshot — restore from an existing snapshot
  • Persistent Volume — keep /workspace across recreations
  • Mount Home — persistent home directory across all sandboxes
  • Data Path — mount user data (or subpath) to /data
  • Tailscale — connect to your Tailscale network
  • Temporary — auto-remove when you disconnect

Or use the CLI for quick creation:

Quick throwaway sandbox — deleted when you disconnect:

sandcastle create scratch --rm

Persistent sandbox — keeps your home directory and data across restarts:

sandcastle create my-dev --home --data
Tip: Set environment variables to make flags the default:
export SANDCASTLE_HOME=1 SANDCASTLE_DATA=. SANDCASTLE_RM=1
<%# ── Connect ── %>

5. Connect

Two ways to get a shell:

# tmux session (reconnectable, auto-starts stopped sandboxes)
sandcastle connect my-dev

# Plain SSH
sandcastle ssh my-dev
<%# ── Web Terminal ── %>

6. Web terminal

Click tmux or shell on any running sandbox to open a browser-based terminal. No SSH client needed — just a web browser.

How it works: ttyd runs inside each sandbox container, exposing two terminal endpoints: tmux (persistent, re-attachable session shared across browser tabs) and shell (a plain login shell). Both are protected by your existing login — no extra passwords required.
<%# ── Browser ── %>

7. Web browser

Click the Browser button on any running sandbox to access Google Chrome with a graphical desktop environment. Perfect for browser automation, visual testing, or running GUI applications.

How it works: Each sandbox includes Google Chrome, Xvfb (virtual X server), and x11vnc. Clicking Browser launches a noVNC container that connects to the sandbox's VNC server, giving you a full graphical browser in your web browser.
Chrome profile persistence: Go to Settings → Browser to enable Chrome profile persistence. When enabled, your Chrome extensions, bookmarks, and settings are saved and persist across sandbox recreations. If you use Mount Home, profiles are stored in ~/.config/google-chrome alongside all your other home directory files. Without Mount Home, profiles are stored separately in a dedicated directory.
<%# ── Manage ── %>

8. Manage sandboxes

sandcastle list              # Show all your sandboxes
sandcastle stop my-dev       # Stop a running sandbox
sandcastle start my-dev      # Start a stopped sandbox
sandcastle delete my-dev     # Permanently remove a sandbox
<%# ── Routes ── %>

9. Custom domain routes

Expose services running inside your sandbox on custom domains with automatic TLS. Each sandbox can have multiple routes pointing to different ports: 

# Add a route — app.example.com → port 8080 (default) in sandbox "myapp"
sandcastle route add myapp app.example.com

# Add another route on a custom port
sandcastle route add myapp api.example.com 3000

# List all routes for a sandbox
sandcastle route list myapp

# Remove a specific route
sandcastle route delete myapp app.example.com

Point a DNS A/CNAME record at <%= host %> before adding the route. TLS certificates are provisioned automatically via Let's Encrypt.

<%# ── Snapshots ── %>

10. Snapshots

Save and restore the full state of a sandbox. Snapshots are composite — they capture the container filesystem (via docker commit) and, when on a BTRFS filesystem, also the home directory, data directory, and workspace volume. 

# Create a snapshot (all available layers)
sandcastle snapshot create my-dev before-migration

# Create with a description and specific layers
sandcastle snapshot create my-dev checkpoint --label "before big refactor" --layers container,home

# List all snapshots
sandcastle snapshot list

# Show details of a specific snapshot
sandcastle snapshot show before-migration

# Restore a snapshot into an existing sandbox (all layers)
sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration

# Restore only the container layer
sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration --layers container

# Create a new sandbox from a snapshot (all layers)
sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration

# Clone only the container layer into a new sandbox
sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration --restore-layers container

# Delete a snapshot
sandcastle snapshot delete before-migration

Web UI: Each sandbox has a detail page (click the sandbox name) with a Snapshots tab for creating, viewing, and deleting snapshots. The global <%= link_to "Snapshots page", snapshots_path, class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %> shows all snapshots across all your sandboxes with clone-to-new-sandbox support.

<% unless (BtrfsHelper.btrfs? rescue false) %>
Note: BTRFS not detected on this host. Only the container layer is snapshotted. Home, data, and workspace layers require BTRFS.
<% end %>
<%# ── Settings ── %>

11. User settings

Click Settings in the navbar to manage your account preferences:

  • Profile — Update your email address and change your password
  • Tailscale — View connection status and toggle auto-connect for new sandboxes
  • Browser — Toggle Chrome profile persistence across sandbox recreations
  • API Tokens — Create, list, and revoke multiple named API tokens for different devices or applications
Using API tokens: Create tokens for different use cases (laptop, CI/CD, mobile).
Via Web UI: Go to Settings → click "New Token" → give it a name → copy the token
Via CLI: Run sandcastle token create laptop to create a named token
sandcastle token list to view all your tokens
sandcastle token revoke <id> to revoke a specific token
<%# ── Inviting Users (Admin) ── %>

12. Inviting users (admin)

Admins can invite new users via email. Each invite generates a unique, one-time registration link. New users click the link to choose a username and set a password — no pre-created account required.

To send an invite:

  1. Go to Admin → Invites
  2. Click Invite User
  3. Enter the invitee's email address and an optional personal message
  4. Click Send Invite — the invitee will receive an email with a registration link

Invite lifecycle:

  • Pending — sent but not yet accepted; link is still valid
  • Accepted — user has registered; link is invalidated
  • Expired — link expired (default: 7 days) before being accepted

Pending invites can be revoked from the Invites admin panel at any time. Once revoked, the registration link immediately becomes invalid.

<%# ── Custom SSL Certificates ── %>

13. Custom SSL certificates (advanced)

For internal networks, you can configure alternative hostnames with custom SSL certificates to avoid browser warnings.

Generate certificates with mkcert:

# Install mkcert (macOS example)
brew install mkcert
mkcert -install

# Generate certificate for your domains
mkcert sandcastle.internal sc.local

Configure Sandcastle:

Add these variables to your sandcastle.env before installation:

SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES=sandcastle.internal,sc.local
REDACTED.internal+1.pem
REDACTED.internal+1-key.pem
SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE=selfsigned

content.thinking: The user is asking to explain what the routes do in the context of the Sandcastle project. They likely have a typo ("whet" instead of "what" and "routs" instead of "routes"). Let me explain what routes are and what they do in Sandcastle.

content.text:
Routes let you expose a service running inside a sandbox on a public URL with automatic TLS.

**Without routes**, a sandbox is only reachable via SSH (or the web terminal/VNC). The services running inside — a web app on port 3000, an API on port 8080, etc. — have no way to receive external HTTP traffic.

**With a route**, you map a public domain to a container port:

```
app.example.com  →  sandbox container port 3000
api.example.com  →  sandbox container port 8080
```

Traefik (the reverse proxy in front of everything) picks up a dynamically generated YAML config file and starts forwarding HTTPS requests for that domain directly into the container over the internal Docker network. Let's Encrypt TLS is handled automatically.

**Lifecycle behaviour:**
- Routes are persisted in the DB — they survive sandbox restarts
- When the sandbox **stops**, the Traefik config is removed (routes go dark)
- When the sandbox **starts** again, routes are automatically reconnected
- When the sandbox is **destroyed**, routes are deleted entirely

**What the web UI adds** is the ability to manage this without the CLI — you can add a domain + port and delete existing routes directly from the sandbox detail page. The sandbox must be running to add a route (because RouteManager also connects the container to the Traefik Docker network at that point), but you can delete routes regardless of status.

message.content: can traffic do ssh multi-home or only https?

content.thinking:
The user is asking about whether Traefik can handle SSH traffic (TCP) in addition to HTTPS. They're asking about "SSH multi-home" which I think means whether Traefik can route SSH connections to multiple sandboxes based on some criteria (like SNI or port).

Let me think about this in the context of Sandcastle:

Currently, SSH access to sandboxes requires Tailscale (as shown in `SandboxManager#connect_info` - it raises an error if Tailscale isn't enabled). The sandboxes have SSH ports in the range 2201-2299 that are bound to the host, but those are only used internally.


Traefik can handle TCP routing including SSH, but the practical options are limited here. Port-based routing would just replicate what's already happening with Docker's port bindings, and SNI-based routing won't work since SSH doesn't use TLS natively. So while Traefik technically supports TCP routing, it wouldn't really solve the multi-home problem for SSH without some additional layer like wrapping connections in TLS or using a different routing mechanism. with `HostSNI` rules, but this requires TLS wrapping (SSH over TLS), which standard SSH clients don't do. For plain SSH, port-based routing doesn't scale well since you'd need one port per sandbox, and there's no way to do hostname-based SSH routing with standard `ssh` clients because SSH doesn't send a hostname header.

For Sandcastle specifically, Traefik handles HTTPS for routes, terminals, and VNC, while SSH access goes through either direct host ports (2201-2299, though not publicly exposed) or Tailscale via the `connect_info` method. Traefik can technically do TCP routing, but it can't handle multi-home SSH routing through a single port without TLS wrapping—standard SSH clients just connect to a specific IP and port with no hostname metadata for Traefik to route on.

content.text:
Traefik can handle TCP (including SSH), but **hostname-based SSH routing isn't possible** with standard SSH clients — here's why:

**HTTPS routing works** because TLS has SNI — the client sends the hostname *before* the encrypted handshake, so Traefik can read `app.example.com` and route accordingly, all on port 443.

**SSH has no equivalent**. A raw SSH connection opens with the server's protocol banner (`SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.x`) — there's no hostname field. Traefik has no information to route on, so it can't distinguish `sandbox-a` from `sandbox-b` on the same port.

**What Traefik *can* do for TCP/SSH:**

- **Port-based routing** — one dedicated port per sandbox. That's essentially what Sandcastle already does (ports 2201–2299 bound directly on the host via Docker). Traefik would just be a pass-through middleman, adding no value.
- **SNI-based TCP routing** — works only if SSH is wrapped in TLS first (e.g. `ssh` over a TLS tunnel). Standard `ssh` clients don't do this.

**So the current Sandcastle architecture is correct:**
- HTTP/S traffic → Traefik → container (routes, terminal, VNC)
- SSH → Tailscale → container directly (no proxy needed, no port collision problem)

If you wanted public SSH without Tailscale, the realistic options are a jump host / `ProxyCommand` approach, or something like [sslh](https://github.com/yrutschle/sslh) that multiplexes SSH+HTTPS on port 443 — but none of that is hostname-aware, so you still can't share a single port across multiple sandboxes.

message.content: explain SNI-based TCP routing — works only if SSH is wrapped in TLS first (e.g. ssh over a TLS tunnel). Standard ssh clients don't do this.

content.thinking: The user wants a deeper explanation of SNI-based TCP routing and why SSH over TLS would be needed. Let me explain this clearly.

content.text:
**SNI (Server Name Indication)** is a TLS extension added in the ClientHello — the very first packet a TLS client sends. Before any encryption happens, the client announces which hostname it wants:

```
Client → Server:  "ClientHello, I want to talk to api.example.com"
Server → Client:  "Here's the certificate for api.example.com"
... encrypted from here ...
```

Traefik sits in front and reads that SNI field *without decrypting anything*. It just peeks at the hostname and forwards the raw TCP stream to the right backend. The backend terminates TLS itself. This is called **TLS passthrough**.

```
Browser ──TLS(SNI=api.example.com)──▶ Traefik ──▶ sandbox-a:443
Browser ──TLS(SNI=db.example.com)───▶ Traefik ──▶ sandbox-b:443
```

Same port (443), different destinations, zero decryption by Traefik.

---

**Why SSH can't use this directly**

A normal SSH connection looks like this on the wire:

```
Client → Server:  (TCP connect)
Server → Client:  "SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.7\r\n"   ← server speaks first
Client → Server:  "SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.3\r\n"   ← client replies
... key exchange ...
```

There is no hostname in any of this. The client just connected to an IP and port — it never said *which* server it wanted. Traefik has nothing to route on.

---

**SSH over TLS would fix it**, but requires a wrapper on both ends:

```
ssh → stunnel/socat (wraps in TLS with SNI=sandbox-a.example.com)
         │
         ▼
      Traefik (reads SNI, routes TCP stream)
         │
         ▼
      stunnel/socat in sandbox (unwraps TLS, hands plain TCP to sshd)
```

The client side would need something like:
```
# ~/.ssh/config
Host sandbox-a.example.com
  ProxyCommand openssl s_client -quiet -connect %h:443 -servername %h
```

This works but it's awkward — every user needs the ProxyCommand configured, the sandbox needs a TLS unwrapper running alongside sshd, and the latency adds up (TLS handshake on top of SSH handshake).

---

**Bottom line**: SNI routing is elegant for HTTPS because TLS is already there. Retrofitting it onto SSH adds complexity with no real benefit over Tailscale, which gives you direct, authenticated, encrypted connectivity without any proxy at all.

message.content: okay. so i want HTTPS forwarding with SNI and add a mode for a "normal" port forward that would create a static port on the public traaefik side.

content.thinking:
The user wants two types of routes:

1. **HTTPS/SNI routing** (what's already implemented) — domain-based routing via Traefik with TLS, using `Host()` rules
2. **TCP port forwarding** — a static public port on the Traefik side that forwards TCP traffic to a container port. This would use Traefik's TCP router with a `HostSNI("*")` catchall rule on a dedicated port (entrypoint).

For TCP port forwarding:
- Traefik needs a dedicated **entrypoint** for each port (you can't do dynamic ports in Traefik without restarting it — each entrypoint is defined in the static config)


- But there's a workaround: pre-define a range of ports as entrypoints in the static config, then assign them dynamically via the dynamic config.

Defining entrypoints in `traefik.yml`...

Then in the dynamic config, I can create TCP routers that reference these pre-defined entrypoints and route traffic to the appropriate service. So for the port forwarding approach, I'd pre-allocate a pool of TCP entrypoints (like ports 2300-2399) in the static config, then dynamically assign them to sandboxes as needed.

The Route model would need a `mode` field to distinguish between HTTP and TCP routes, plus a `public_port` field for TCP mode that gets assigned from the available pool. This also means I need to handle port pool management to track which ones are in use, update firewall rules to allow the TCP port range, and add the necessary database columns. It's a substantial change to the architecture, so I should think through the implications before implementing. rypoints. The installer would need to add these to `traefik.yml`.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The user said "add a mode for a 'normal' port forward that would create a static port on the public Traefik side." So they want:

- Mode 1: **HTTP/HTTPS** (existing) - domain-based with SNI, port 443
- Mode 2: **TCP port forward** - a specific public port that Traefik listens on and forwards TCP to the container port

For Mode 2, the implementation needs:
1. A pool of available public ports (pre-configured in Traefik static config)
2. Port allocation when a TCP route is created
3. A `HostSNI("*")` TCP router in Traefik dynamic config

This is a non-trivial change. Given the complexity involved with static config changes, model updates, and port pool management, I should use EnterPlanMode to work through the design properly before diving into implementation.

    10→          to: websecure

    14→

    20→      httpChallenge:
    21→        entryPoint: web

    27→

content.content:
1→class RouteManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def add_route(sandbox:, domain:, port: 8080)
     9→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    10→
    11→    route = sandbox.routes.create!(domain: domain, port: port)
    12→
    13→    ensure_network
    14→    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    15→    write_config(sandbox)
    16→
    17→    route
    18→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    19→    raise Error, e.message
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  def remove_route(route:)
    23→    sandbox = route.sandbox
    24→    route.destroy!
    25→
    26→    if sandbox.routes.reload.any?
    27→      write_config(sandbox)
    28→    else
    29→      delete_config(sandbox)
    30→      disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    31→    end
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  def remove_all_routes(sandbox:)
    35→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    36→
    37→    sandbox.routes.destroy_all
    38→    delete_config(sandbox)
    39→    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  def suspend_routes(sandbox:)
    43→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    44→
    45→    delete_config(sandbox)
    46→    disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
    47→  end
    48→
    49→  def reconnect_routes(sandbox:)
    50→    return unless sandbox.routed?
    51→
    52→    ensure_network
    53→    connect_to_network(sandbox)
    54→    write_config(sandbox)
    55→  end
    56→
    57→  def sync_all_configs
    58→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    59→
    60→    active_ids = Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.pluck(:id).to_set
    61→
    62→    # Remove stale config files
    63→    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-*.yml")).each do |path|
    64→      id = File.basename(path, ".yml").delete_prefix("sandbox-").to_i
    65→      unless active_ids.include?(id)
    66→        File.delete(path)
    67→        Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: removed stale config #{File.basename(path)}")
    68→      end
    69→    end
    70→
    71→    # Regenerate configs for active routed sandboxes
    72→    Sandbox.active.running.joins(:routes).distinct.includes(:user, :routes).find_each do |sandbox|
    73→      ensure_network
    74→      connect_to_network(sandbox)
    75→      write_config(sandbox)
    76→    end
    77→  end
    78→
    79→  def write_rails_config(host:)
    80→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    81→
    82→    # Build host list: main host + optional alternative hostnames
    83→    hosts = [ host ]
    84→    alt_hostnames = ENV["SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES"].to_s.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
    85→    hosts += alt_hostnames
    86→
    87→    host_rules = hosts.map { |h| "`#{h}`" }.join(", ")
    88→    rule = "Host(#{host_rules})"
    89→
    90→    config = {
    91→      "http" => {
    92→        "routers" => {
    93→          "rails-http" => {
    94→            "rule" => rule,
    95→            "service" => "rails",
    96→            "entryPoints" => [ "web" ]
    97→          },
    98→          "rails-https" => {
    99→            "rule" => rule,
   100→            "service" => "rails",
   101→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
   102→            "tls" => tls_config
   103→          }
   104→        },
   105→        "services" => {
   106→          "rails" => {
   107→            "loadBalancer" => {
   108→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://sandcastle-web:80" } ]
   109→            }
   110→          }
   111→        }
   112→      }
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "rails.yml"), config.to_yaml)
   116→    write_tls_config
   117→  end
   118→
   119→  private
   120→
   121→  SELFSIGNED_MODES = %w[selfsigned mkcert].freeze
   122→
   123→  def write_tls_config
   124→    tls_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "tls.yml")
   125→
   126→    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
   127→      # Traefik-perspective path (referenced inside tls.yml)
   128→      cert_dir = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TLS_CERT_DIR", "/data/certs")
   129→      # Rails-perspective path (where we can actually write files)
   130→      local_cert_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs")
   131→
   132→      case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
   133→      when "selfsigned" then ensure_selfsigned_cert(local_cert_dir)
   134→      when "mkcert"     then ensure_mkcert_cert(local_cert_dir)
   135→      end
   136→
   137→      certs = [ { "certFile" => "#{cert_dir}/cert.pem", "keyFile" => "#{cert_dir}/key.pem" } ]
   138→
   139→      if custom_cert_configured?
   140→        certs << { "certFile" => "/data/certs/custom-cert.pem", "keyFile" => "/data/certs/custom-key.pem" }
   141→      end
   142→
   143→      File.write(tls_path, { "tls" => { "certificates" => certs } }.to_yaml)
   144→    else
   145→      File.delete(tls_path) if File.exist?(tls_path)
   146→    end
   147→  end
   148→
   149→  def ensure_selfsigned_cert(cert_dir)
   150→    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
   151→    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
   152→    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   153→
   154→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
   155→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating self-signed certificate for #{host}")
   156→
   157→    key  = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.generate(4096)
   158→    cert = OpenSSL::X509::Certificate.new
   159→    cert.version    = 2
   160→    cert.serial     = OpenSSL::BN.rand(128)
   161→    cert.subject    = OpenSSL::X509::Name.parse("/CN=#{host}")
   162→    cert.issuer     = cert.subject
   163→    cert.public_key = key.public_key
   164→    cert.not_before = Time.now
   165→    cert.not_after  = Time.now + 10 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60
   166→
   167→    ef = OpenSSL::X509::ExtensionFactory.new
   168→    ef.subject_certificate = cert
   169→    ef.issuer_certificate  = cert
   170→
   171→    san = host.match?(/\A[\d.]+\z/) ? "IP:#{host}" : "DNS:#{host}"
   172→    san += ",IP:127.0.0.1,DNS:localhost"
   173→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("subjectAltName", san))
   174→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("basicConstraints", "CA:FALSE", true))
   175→    cert.add_extension(ef.create_extension("keyUsage", "digitalSignature,keyEncipherment", true))
   176→    cert.sign(key, OpenSSL::Digest::SHA256.new)
   177→
   178→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
   179→    File.write(key_path,  key.to_pem,  perm: 0o600)
   180→    File.write(cert_path, cert.to_pem, perm: 0o644)
   181→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: self-signed certificate written to #{cert_path}")
   182→  end
   183→
   184→  def ensure_mkcert_cert(cert_dir)
   185→    cert_path = File.join(cert_dir, "cert.pem")
   186→    key_path  = File.join(cert_dir, "key.pem")
   187→    return if File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   188→
   189→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
   190→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: generating mkcert certificate for #{host}")
   191→
   192→    # Store CA alongside the certs so it survives container restarts
   193→    caroot = ENV.fetch("MKCERT_CAROOT", cert_dir)
   194→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(cert_dir)
   195→
   196→    env = { "CAROOT" => caroot }
   197→    system(
   198→      env,
   199→      "mkcert",
   200→      "-cert-file", cert_path,
   201→      "-key-file",  key_path,
   202→      host, "*.#{host}", "localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"
   203→    ) or raise Error, "mkcert certificate generation failed for #{host}"
   204→
   205→    ca_source = File.join(caroot, "rootCA.pem")
   206→    ca_dest   = File.join(cert_dir, "rootCA.pem")
   207→    FileUtils.cp(ca_source, ca_dest) if File.exist?(ca_source) && File.expand_path(ca_source) != File.expand_path(ca_dest)
   208→
   209→    Rails.logger.info("RouteManager: mkcert certificate written to #{cert_path}")
   210→  end
   211→
   212→  def custom_cert_configured?
   213→    cert_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-cert.pem")
   214→    key_path = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "certs", "custom-key.pem")
   215→    File.exist?(cert_path) && File.exist?(key_path)
   216→  end
   217→
   218→  def config_path(sandbox)
   219→    File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
   220→  end
   221→
   222→  def write_config(sandbox)
   223→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
   224→
   225→    routes = sandbox.routes.reload
   226→    routers = {}
   227→    services = {}
   228→
   229→    routes.each do |route|
   230→      key = "sandbox-#{sandbox.id}-r#{route.id}"
   231→      routers[key] = {
   232→        "rule" => "Host(`#{route.domain}`)",
   233→        "service" => key,
   234→        "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
   235→        "tls" => tls_config
   236→      }
   237→      services[key] = {
   238→        "loadBalancer" => {
   239→          "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{sandbox.full_name}:#{route.port}" } ]
   240→        }
   241→      }
   242→    end
   243→
   244→    config = { "http" => { "routers" => routers, "services" => services } }
   245→    File.write(config_path(sandbox), config.to_yaml)
   246→  end
   247→
   248→  def tls_config
   249→    if SELFSIGNED_MODES.include?(ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"])
   250→      {}
   251→    else
   252→      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
   253→    end
   254→  end
   255→
   256→  def delete_config(sandbox)
   257→    path = config_path(sandbox)
   258→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   259→  end
   260→
   261→  def ensure_network
   262→    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   263→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   264→    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
   265→  end
   266→
   267→  def connect_to_network(sandbox)
   268→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→
   270→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   271→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   272→
   273→    # Check if already connected
   274→    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
   275→    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)
   276→
   277→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   278→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   279→    raise Error, "Failed to connect container to network: #{e.message}"
   280→  end
   281→
   282→  def disconnect_from_network(sandbox)
   283→    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   284→
   285→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   286→    network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   287→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   288→    # Network or container already gone
   289→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   290→    # Container may not be connected
   291→  end
   292→end
   293→

     2→  before_action :set_sandbox
     3→
     4→  def create
     5→    RouteManager.new.add_route(
     6→      sandbox: @sandbox,
     7→      domain: params.require(:route).require(:domain).strip,
     8→      port: params.require(:route).fetch(:port, 8080).to_i

    10→    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), notice: "Route added."

    19→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    20→    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), alert: "Route not found."
    21→  rescue RouteManager::Error => e
    22→    redirect_to sandbox_path(@sandbox), alert: e.message
    23→  end
    24→
    25→  private
    26→

    28→    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:sandbox_id])
    29→    authorize @sandbox, :show?
    30→  end

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}
    32→
    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→Environment:
    66→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    67→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    68→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    69→
    70→Workflow:
    71→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    72→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    73→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    74→USAGE
    75→  exit 0
    76→fi
    77→
    78→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    79→
    80→load_env() {
    81→  local script_dir
    82→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    83→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    84→
    85→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    86→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    87→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
    88→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
    89→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
    90→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
    91→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
    92→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
    93→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
    94→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
    95→  fi
    96→
    97→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
    98→    set -a
    99→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   100→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"

1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      persistent_volume: persistent,
    15→      mount_home: mount_home,
    16→      data_path: data_path,
    17→      temporary: temporary
    18→    )
    19→
    20→    if persistent
    21→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    25→    sandbox.validate!
    26→
    27→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    28→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    29→
    30→    # Now safe to save
    31→    sandbox.save!
    32→
    33→    # Pull image
    34→    ensure_image(image)
    35→
    36→    # Create and start container
    37→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    40→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    41→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    sandbox
    45→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    46→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    47→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    48→  rescue => e
    49→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    50→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    51→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    52→    raise Error, e.message
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  # Public method for job usage
    56→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    57→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    58→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    59→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    60→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    61→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    62→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    63→      "HostConfig" => {
    64→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    65→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    66→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    67→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    68→      },
    69→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    70→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    71→      }
    72→    )
    73→
    74→    container.start
    75→    container.refresh!
    76→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    77→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    78→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    79→    end
    80→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    81→
    82→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    83→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    84→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    85→  end
    86→
    87→  # Public method for job usage
    88→  def ensure_image(image)
    89→    Docker::Image.get(image)
    90→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    91→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    92→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
    93→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    94→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    95→  end
    96→
    97→  # Public method for job usage
    98→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    99→    # Directories bind-mounted into Sysbox containers must be world-writable
   100→    # because Sysbox maps container root to a high host UID (via /etc/subuid)
   101→    # that won't match the directory owner.
   102→
   103→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   104→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   105→
   106→    if sandbox.mount_home
   107→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   108→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   109→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   110→    end
   111→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   112→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   113→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   114→
   115→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   116→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   117→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   118→    end
   119→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   120→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(sandbox.volume_path)
   121→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, sandbox.volume_path)
   122→    end
   123→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   124→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   125→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   126→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   127→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   128→    end
   129→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   130→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   131→  end
   132→
   133→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false)
   134→    begin
   135→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   136→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   137→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   138→    end
   139→
   140→    begin
   141→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   142→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   143→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   144→    end
   145→
   146→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   147→
   148→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   149→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   150→    end
   151→
   152→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   153→      begin
   154→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   155→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   156→        container.delete(force: true)
   157→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   158→        # Container already gone
   159→      end
   160→    end
   161→
   162→    unless keep_volume
   163→      FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   164→    end
   165→
   166→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   167→  end
   168→
   169→  def start(sandbox:)
   170→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   171→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   172→
   173→    user = sandbox.user
   174→
   175→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   176→      begin
   177→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   178→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   179→        old.delete(force: true)
   180→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   181→        # already gone
   182→      end
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   186→
   187→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   188→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   189→
   190→    sandbox
   191→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   192→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   193→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def stop(sandbox:)
   197→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   198→
   199→    begin
   200→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   201→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   202→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   203→    end
   204→
   205→    begin
   206→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   207→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   208→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   209→    end
   210→
   211→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   212→
   213→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   214→      begin
   215→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   216→        container.stop(t: 10)
   217→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   218→        # already gone
   219→      end
   220→    end
   221→
   222→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   223→    sandbox
   224→  end
   225→
   226→  def status(sandbox:)
   227→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   228→
   229→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   230→    info = container.json
   231→    {
   232→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   233→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   234→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   235→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   236→    }
   237→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   238→    { state: "not_found" }
   239→  end
   240→
   241→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   242→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   243→  #
   244→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   245→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   246→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   247→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   248→
   249→    user = sandbox.user
   250→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   251→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   252→
   253→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   254→      user: user,
   255→      name: name,
   256→      label: label,
   257→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   258→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   259→    )
   260→
   261→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   262→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   263→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   264→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   265→      image = container.commit(
   266→        repo: repo,
   267→        tag: name,
   268→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   269→      )
   270→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   271→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   272→    end
   273→
   274→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   275→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   276→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   277→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   278→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   279→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   280→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   281→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   282→      end
   283→    end
   284→
   285→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   286→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   287→      if data_subdir.present?
   288→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   289→      else
   290→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   291→      end
   292→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   293→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   294→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   295→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   296→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   297→      end
   298→    end
   299→
   300→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   301→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   302→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   303→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   304→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   305→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   306→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   307→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   308→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   309→      end
   310→    end
   311→
   312→    snap.save!
   313→    snap
   314→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   315→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   316→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   317→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   318→  end
   319→
   320→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   321→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   322→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   323→    {
   324→      name: snap.name,
   325→      image: snap.docker_image,
   326→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   327→      created_at: snap.created_at
   328→    }
   329→  end
   330→
   331→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   332→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   333→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   334→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   335→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   336→  end
   337→
   338→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   339→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   340→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   341→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   342→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   343→  end
   344→
   345→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   346→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   347→
   348→    if snap
   349→      # Remove Docker image
   350→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   351→        begin
   352→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   353→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   354→          # Already gone
   355→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   356→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   357→        end
   358→      end
   359→
   360→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   361→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   362→        begin
   363→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   364→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   365→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   366→        end
   367→      end
   368→
   369→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   370→        begin
   371→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   372→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   373→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   374→        end
   375→      end
   376→
   377→      snap.destroy!
   378→    else
   379→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   380→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   381→      begin
   382→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   383→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   384→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   385→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   386→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   387→      end
   388→    end
   389→  end
   390→
   391→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   392→    user = sandbox.user
   393→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   394→
   395→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   396→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   397→
   398→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   399→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   400→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   401→    else
   402→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   403→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   404→    end
   405→
   406→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   407→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   408→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   409→
   410→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   411→    if restore_container
   412→      begin
   413→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   414→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   415→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   416→      end
   417→    end
   418→
   419→    begin
   420→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   421→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   422→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   423→    end
   424→
   425→    begin
   426→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   427→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   428→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   429→    end
   430→
   431→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   432→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   433→    end
   434→
   435→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   436→      begin
   437→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   438→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   439→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   440→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   441→        # Already gone
   442→      end
   443→    end
   444→
   445→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   446→    if restore_home
   447→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   448→      begin
   449→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   450→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   451→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   452→      end
   453→    end
   454→
   455→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   456→    if restore_data
   457→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   458→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   459→      else
   460→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   461→      end
   462→      begin
   463→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   464→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   465→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   466→      end
   467→    end
   468→
   469→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   470→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   471→
   472→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   473→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   474→      "Image" => final_image,
   475→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   476→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   477→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   478→      "HostConfig" => {
   479→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   480→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   481→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   482→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   483→      },
   484→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   485→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   486→      }
   487→    )
   488→
   489→    container.start
   490→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   491→
   492→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   493→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   494→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   495→
   496→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   497→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   498→    end
   499→
   500→    sandbox
   501→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   502→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   503→  end
   504→
   505→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   506→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   507→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   508→
   509→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   510→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   511→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   512→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   513→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   514→        next if tag_name.blank?
   515→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   516→
   517→        Snapshot.create!(
   518→          user: user,
   519→          name: tag_name,
   520→          docker_image: tag,
   521→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   522→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   523→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   524→        )
   525→      end
   526→    end
   527→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   528→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   529→  end
   530→
   531→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   532→    {
   533→      name: snap.name,
   534→      label: snap.label,
   535→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   536→      layers: snap.layers,
   537→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   538→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   539→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   540→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   541→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   542→      created_at: snap.created_at
   543→    }
   544→  end
   545→
   546→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   547→    user = sandbox.user.name
   548→
   549→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   550→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   551→    end
   552→
   553→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   554→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   555→
   556→    {
   557→      host: ts_ip,
   558→      port: 22,
   559→      user: user,
   560→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   561→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   562→    }
   563→  end
   564→
   565→  private
   566→
   567→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   568→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   569→    max_attempts.times do
   570→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   571→
   572→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   573→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   574→        sleep delay
   575→        next
   576→      end
   577→
   578→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   579→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   580→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   581→
   582→      sleep delay
   583→    end
   584→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   585→    nil
   586→  end
   587→
   588→  def connect_to_network(container)
   589→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   590→    network.connect(container.id)
   591→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   592→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   593→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   594→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   595→  end
   596→
   597→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   598→    env = [
   599→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   600→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   601→    ]
   602→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   603→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   604→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   605→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   606→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   607→    env
   608→  end
   609→
   610→  def container_runtime
   611→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   612→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   613→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   614→        "sysbox-runc"
   615→      else
   616→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   617→        "runc"
   618→      end
   619→    end
   620→  end
   621→
   622→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   623→    binds = []
   624→    if sandbox.mount_home
   625→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   626→    end
   627→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   628→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   629→    end
   630→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   631→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   632→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   633→    end
   634→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   635→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   636→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   637→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   638→    end
   639→    binds
   640→  end
   641→end
   642→
